prompt: Write a 150-word short story about a lighthouse keeper who discovers something unexpected in the fog. Do not use dialogue.
response: As the thick morning fog crawled ashore, the lighthouse keeper tended to his routine, winding the beacon with practiced care. Waves whispered secrets below, but today the silence grew heavy, dense as the swirl outside his window. Peering through the mist, his eyes caught movement—a shape, drifting closer. Heart quickening, he grabbed his lantern and descended the spiral stairs. On the rocky beach, he found a rowboat gently bumping the shore, its hull marked with strange symbols. Bundled inside was a weathered journal, pages brittle with brine, and a silver locket glinting between folds of sodden cloth. The pages revealed sketches of the very lighthouse he kept, dated decades before his own arrival. Carefully, he cradled the locket, sensing the weight of forgotten memories. The fog thinned, sunlight bathing the stones. Clutching the journal, he realized he was not the lighthouse’s only guardian—stories lost to time awaited beneath the ever-turning beam.
